req.title: Bulk data export (boundary)
req.statement: A provisioned client shall request a FHIR bulk export; large datasets shall be paginated with NDJSON output. Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates FHIR bulk export via /$export with NDJSON output, pagination for large datasets, boundary handling on timestamps and numeric elements, and a 403 for unauthorized clients. Four tests are designed: (1) EP positive – authorized client receives 202 + Content-Location header confirming job queued; (2) NEG negative – unauthorized client receives 403; (3) BVA boundary – _since parameter set to epoch boundary timestamp (1970-01-01T00:00:00Z) exercises minimum datetime edge; (4) BVA boundary – numeric element at max safe integer boundary in NDJSON output. State Transition Coverage is applied to a fifth test verifying the job transitions from accepted → completed and delivers valid NDJSON. Tests use placeholder fixtures and helpers to stay within the 40-line limit and remain deterministic.

tests.name: test_bulk_export_authorized_client_queues_job
preconditions: Client is provisioned and holds a valid bearer token
actions: Send GET /$export with Accept: application/fhir+json and Prefer: respond-async headers using the authorized token
Capture HTTP response status and Content-Location header
expected: Response contains a Content-Location header pointing to a polling URL
trace: R-HLT-041: export job queued on authorized request

tests.name: test_bulk_export_unauthorized_client_returns_403
preconditions: No valid bearer token is present (missing Authorization header)
actions: Send GET /$export with Accept: application/fhir+json and Prefer: respond-async headers but WITHOUT an Authorization header
expected: Response body contains a FHIR OperationOutcome with severity error
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8080/fhir"

def test_bulk_export_unauthorized_client_returns_403():
    resp = requests.get(
        f"{BASE_URL}/$export",
        headers={
            "Accept": "application/fhir+json",
            "Prefer": "respond-async",
        },
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403, f"Expected 403, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    resource_type = body.get("resourceType", "")
    assert resource_type == "OperationOutcome", f"Expected OperationOutcome, got {resource_type}"
    issues = body.get("issue", [])
    assert any(i.get("severity") == "error" for i in issues), "No error-severity issue found"

tests.name: test_bulk_export_since_epoch_boundary_timestamp
preconditions: Client is provisioned and holds a valid bearer token
At least one resource exists with lastUpdated >= 1970-01-01T00:00:00Z
actions: Capture HTTP response status and Content-Location header
expected: Content-Location header is present (job is accepted even at epoch boundary)
trace: R-HLT-041: boundary cases on timestamps must be tested
BVA lower boundary for FHIR instant datatype

tests.name: test_bulk_export_ndjson_numeric_max_safe_integer_boundary
preconditions: Client is provisioned and holds a valid bearer token
A Patient resource exists with an extension carrying a numeric value equal to 9007199254740991 (2^53-1, JS max safe integer)
actions: Fetch the first NDJSON output file
Parse each NDJSON line and locate the numeric extension value
expected: Numeric value 9007199254740991 is preserved exactly without rounding
trace: R-HLT-041: boundary cases on numeric elements must be tested
BVA upper boundary for IEEE-754 double precision safe integer range
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
import json
import time

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8080/fhir"
MAX_SAFE_INT = 9007199254740991

@pytest.fixture
def auth_headers():
    return <fixture_authorized_bulk_client_headers>

def test_bulk_export_ndjson_numeric_max_safe_integer_boundary(auth_headers):
    resp = requests.get(
        f"{BASE_URL}/$export",
        params={"_type": "Patient"},
        headers={**auth_headers, "Accept": "application/fhir+json", "Prefer": "respond-async"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 202
    poll_url = resp.headers["Content-Location"]
    manifest = <helper_fn_poll_until_complete>(poll_url, auth_headers, timeout=30)
    assert manifest.get("output"), "No output files in manifest"
    ndjson_url = manifest["output"][0]["url"]
    ndjson_resp = requests.get(ndjson_url, headers=auth_headers)
    assert ndjson_resp.status_code == 200
    found = False
    for line in ndjson_resp.text.splitlines():
        resource = json.loads(line)
        for ext in resource.get("extension", []):
            if ext.get("valueInteger") == MAX_SAFE_INT or ext.get("valueDecimal") == MAX_SAFE_INT:
                found = True
    assert found, f"Max safe integer {MAX_SAFE_INT} not found or was rounded in NDJSON output"

tests.name: test_bulk_export_job_state_transition_accepted_to_completed
preconditions: Client is provisioned and holds a valid bearer token
Dataset contains at least one Patient resource
actions: Send GET /$export?_type=Patient to initiate job (state: initial → accepted)
Poll Content-Location URL until X-Progress changes from 'in-progress' to absent and status is 200 (state: accepted → in-progress → completed)
Verify final response is a Bulk Data manifest JSON
expected: Initial response is 202 with Content-Location (accepted state)
Polling returns 202 with X-Progress header while in-progress
Final poll returns 200 with a JSON manifest containing 'output' array (completed state)
Each output entry specifies 'type' and 'url' fields
trace: R-HLT-041: export job queued; NDJSON stream produced
FHIR Async Bulk Data IG – job lifecycle state machine
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
import time

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8080/fhir"
POLL_INTERVAL = 2
TIMEOUT = 60

@pytest.fixture
def auth_headers():
    return <fixture_authorized_bulk_client_headers>

def test_bulk_export_job_state_transition_accepted_to_completed(auth_headers):
    init = requests.get(
        f"{BASE_URL}/$export",
        params={"_type": "Patient"},
        headers={**auth_headers, "Accept": "application/fhir+json", "Prefer": "respond-async"},
    )
    assert init.status_code == 202, "Job not accepted"
    poll_url = init.headers["Content-Location"]
    deadline = time.time() + TIMEOUT
    while time.time() < deadline:
        poll = requests.get(poll_url, headers=auth_headers)
        if poll.status_code == 200:
            manifest = poll.json()
            assert "output" in manifest, "Manifest missing 'output' key"
            for entry in manifest["output"]:
                assert "type" in entry and "url" in entry
            return
        assert poll.status_code == 202, f"Unexpected status {poll.status_code} while polling"
        time.sleep(POLL_INTERVAL)
    pytest.fail("Bulk export job did not complete within timeout")
